name: Pina Colada Cake in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Wash 8 (1 pint) wide mouth canning jars with lids in hot soapy water and let drain, dry, and cool to room temperature.
3. Generously prepare jars with cooking spray and flour. Drain pineapple for 10 minutes, reserving juice.
4. Puree drained pineapple in a blender until smooth.
5. Measure out 1 1/2 cups puree, adding a little reserved juice if necessary to make 1 1/2 cups. Set puree aside.
6. Discard remaining juice.
7. In a mixing bowl, combine applesauce with half of the brown sugar until light and fluffy.
8. Beat in eggs and pineapple puree.
9. Set aside.
10. In another mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and baking soda.
11. Gradually add to pineapple mixture in thirds, beating well with each addition.
12. Stir in coconut.
13. Spoon 1 level cupful of batter into each jar.
14. Carefully wipe rims clean, then place jars on a baking sheet (or they'll tip over) in the center of oven.
15. Bake for 40 minutes.
16. Keep lids in hot water until used.
17. When cakes are done, remove jars from oven one at a time.
18. If rims need cleaning, use moistened paper towel.
19. Carefully put lids and rings in place, then screw tops on tightly shut.
20. Place jars on a wire rack; they will seal as they cool.
